name: Light Resource
description: A limited-use light source that improves visibility, safety, or combat options while consuming battery, fuel, charge, or attention.
design_notes: Light resources create horror tension by making safety temporary and visible. The design should define drain rules, refill transactions, monster interactions, UI warning bands, and whether darkness affects navigation, sanity, stealth, or combat. The implementation should keep illumination, detection, and inventory state synchronized.
design_purpose: Make visibility and safety an active resource decision.
Connect exploration pacing to batteries, fuel, generators, flares, or environmental light.
Create tradeoffs between revealing threats and attracting or repelling them.
player_fantasy: The player feels safer while the light holds, but increasingly exposed as the resource runs down.

parameters.name: light_source_id
parameters.description: Light-emitting tool or fixture controlled by the player or level.

parameters.name: light_fuel_remaining_ms
parameters.description: Remaining active-use window for the light source.

parameters.name: visibility_radius
parameters.description: Readable radius created by the current light profile.

parameters.name: refill_item_id
parameters.description: Inventory item consumed to restore light fuel.

parameters.name: monster_detection_modifier_id
parameters.description: AI perception rule applied while the light is active.

parameters.name: warning_band_id
parameters.description: UI and audio state derived from remaining light fuel.

edge_cases: A refill item is used while the light source is active and draining.
The player swaps, drops, or loses the light while a monster perception modifier is active.
A checkpoint restores fuel but not the active light cone, warning band, or AI visibility state.
A scripted blackout overrides player light without clarifying whether fuel was consumed.
Dynamic shadows or post-processing make the scene unreadable even when visibility_radius is high.
common_bugs: Fuel drains twice because both the item and equipped light update the same resource.
The monster AI reacts to the visual light mesh but not the authoritative light_source_id state.
Low-fuel warnings keep playing after refill because warning_band_id does not reset.
Refill transactions consume inventory before validating capacity or current source type.
Battery state is stored on the scene object and resets after loading a new area.
balancing_notes: Low fuel should create pressure before total darkness, not instantly make navigation impossible.
Refill placement controls exploration confidence; clustered refills reduce fear while sparse refills increase route planning.
If light repels threats, fuel becomes a defensive resource and needs stronger scarcity.
accessibility_notes: Offer contrast, brightness, and reduced darkness settings without removing the resource loop.
Use text, icon shape, and audio alternatives for low-fuel warnings.
Avoid extreme flicker effects or provide a reduced-flicker option.

implementation_notes.unity: Keep light_fuel_remaining_ms and warning_band_id in equipment state, then drive Light components, URP/HDRP effects, UI, and AI perception from committed state. Refill should be an inventory transaction that validates refill_item_id and source compatibility before consuming items.
implementation_notes.godot: Use a light controller node for PointLight2D, Light3D, or shader masks, but store fuel and refill state in a player inventory resource. Emit fuel band changes so UI, audio, and monster perception update together.
implementation_notes.unreal: Represent the light source as an Actor Component with replicated fuel state when needed. AI perception should read an explicit stimulus or Gameplay Tag from light_source_id rather than inferring from rendered components.
implementation_notes.web: For Canvas, Phaser, or Three.js, separate the light mask render from the resource reducer. Persist fuel, active source id, and warning band in save data, and use deterministic drain from gameplay clock rather than frame count.

example_games.title: Alan Wake
example_games.note: Uses light as a survival and combat-facing resource.

example_games.title: Amnesia: The Dark Descent
example_games.note: Uses limited light sources to shape exploration tension.
